content: Northern Trail Outfitters wants to modernize its processes by replacing repetitive manual tasks with automations that combine Flow, RPA, Composer, and APIs. According to Salesforce’s view, what best describes **Hyperautomation** in this context?
options: Using RPA only to remove all human involvement.
Combining multiple automation technologies to orchestrate end‑to‑end business processes.
Replacing all legacy systems with a single ERP.
Running autolaunched flows in batch every night.
Deploying a single Einstein Bot for all channels.

content: An architect needs to explain the difference between **isolated automation** and **hyperautomation** to executives. Which statement is most aligned with Salesforce’s recommended understanding?
options: Isolated automation focuses on bots; hyperautomation focuses only on APIs.
Isolated automation uses Composer; hyperautomation always uses Flow Orchestration.
Isolated automation fixes point problems; hyperautomation connects people, data, and systems across end‑to‑end business journeys.
Isolated automation is always synchronous; hyperautomation is always asynchronous.
Isolated automation runs in the cloud; hyperautomation runs only on‑premises.

content: A company wants to reduce customer service handling time by connecting digital channels, Salesforce Service Cloud, legacy systems without APIs, and real‑time partner integrations. Which combination represents a **typical hyperautomation architecture** in the Salesforce ecosystem?
options: Flow Builder, Flow Orchestration, MuleSoft Composer, MuleSoft RPA, and Anypoint Platform APIs.
Apex and Lightning Web Components only.
Einstein Bots and Digital Engagement only.
MuleSoft APIs only, without any Salesforce automation.

content: A CIO wants to select the right automation technology for each type of task. Which mapping best aligns with Salesforce hyperautomation guidance?
options: Salesforce Flow for every external integration, MuleSoft RPA for reporting.
MuleSoft Composer for SaaS‑to‑SaaS integrations, MuleSoft RPA for systems without APIs, Flow Orchestration to coordinate human and automated steps.
Use RPA for everything, because it is more flexible.
Use REST APIs only for any kind of user interaction.
Use Apex only because it is a full programming language.

content: During an automation maturity review, the team finds dozens of automations built by different departments without governance. Which practice best matches Salesforce’s hyperautomation vision to fix this issue?
options: Centralize everything on a single Apex developer.
Turn off existing automations and start from scratch.
Create an Automation Center of Excellence with design standards, asset catalog, and centralized monitoring.
Move all automations into a single Flow.

content: A consultant is defining a **governance** strategy for hyperautomation. Which element is most important to ensure scalability and reuse?
options: Establish naming conventions, API‑led integration patterns, versioning, and dev/test/prod environments.
Let any user activate automations directly in production.

content: A company wants to implement hyperautomation with a strong focus on **data security**. Which approach is best aligned with good practices?
options: Expose sensitive data in flow logs to help troubleshooting.
Use only generic integration users with administrator access everywhere.
Apply least‑privilege access in integrations, mask sensitive data in logs, and use strong authentication for connections.
Store access tokens in plain text custom fields.

content: While building a hyperautomation roadmap, the business team wants to prioritize initiatives. Which criterion is **most suitable** to decide which processes to automate first?
options: Number of fields on the object.
Combination of business impact (time savings, error reduction) and technical feasibility.
Number of users involved, regardless of value.
Choose only processes that involve RPA.

content: An architect is evaluating the use of **API‑led connectivity** in a hyperautomation initiative. What is the main benefit of this approach for Salesforce + MuleSoft?
options: Providing a single giant API that covers all systems.
Splitting integrations into System, Process, and Experience APIs to promote reuse, governance, and decoupling between channels and systems.
Eliminating the need for any logic in Salesforce Flow.
Forcing all processes to be synchronous.

content: Leadership wants to understand the role of **AI** in Salesforce hyperautomation. Which option represents a typical use of AI in this certification’s context?
options: AI replaces all business rules in flows.
Einstein Next Best Action recommends actions while Flow Orchestration and integrations execute the resulting steps.
AI is used only to build dashboards.
AI is used only in Einstein Bots, without process integration.
AI is mandatory for any automation.

content: Northern Trail Outfitters wants to synchronize contacts between Salesforce and Google Sheets using a **no‑code** integration interface. According to the official documentation, which tool should they use?
In MuleSoft Composer, which element represents the **credentials and URL** used to access a system such as Salesforce, Slack, or NetSuite?
An admin is creating a new MuleSoft Composer flow. After selecting a Salesforce‑based connection, what should they configure as the **first step** of the flow, according to the documentation?​
options: A trigger that defines which Salesforce event will start the flow.
A write action on another system.

content: A Composer flow must run every 15 minutes, regardless of events in external systems. What should be configured?
options: A schedule‑based trigger as the start of the flow.

content: While building a flow in MuleSoft Composer, the admin sees sample data flowing through the steps. What is the primary purpose of this **sample data** feature?
options: Let the user test each change in near real time to validate steps before activation.

content: After configuring triggers and actions in a Composer flow, what should the admin do **before activating the flow in production**, according to guidance?
options: Export the flow to Anypoint Studio.
Test the flow on a single record to validate its behavior.
Compile the flow to Java code.
Convert the flow to a managed template.
Move the flow directly to a production org without tests.

content: A Composer flow is active, but users suspect it is not updating a target system correctly. Where should the admin check executions and related errors first?
options: The flow’s **Flow Details** page in MuleSoft Composer.

content: The Composer home page should help admins start common integrations quickly, like syncing contacts across systems. Which feature can they use to accelerate flow creation?
On the MuleSoft Composer home page, the **Usage Summary** section appears. What is its main purpose?
options: Show only the number of logged‑in users.
Display monthly tasks and credit usage per flow for the organization.

content: A Composer flow copies updated records from Salesforce to a billing system. Which best practice should the admin follow when configuring the actions?
options: Always copy all records from the table, regardless of changes.
Copy only new or changed records since the last run to reduce processing.
Always copy every field, even irrelevant ones.
Avoid filters to keep the flow simple.
Configure multiple triggers for the same event.

content: A critical Composer flow runs important integrations. The team wants to be notified of failures in addition to checking the Flow Details page. Which mechanism can be configured to send error notifications?
options: Post to a Chatter feed by default.
Send emails to a configured address.
Send push notifications to the Salesforce mobile app by default.
Post messages in Slack by default, without setup.

content: A company wants **near real‑time bidirectional sync** between two Salesforce orgs using MuleSoft Composer. What is the minimum number of flows required?
In a Composer integration project, IT requires all testing to be done in **sandbox orgs** first. During testing, which Salesforce connections should point to sandboxes?
options: Both the trigger and all actions that interact with Salesforce.
Only actions that write to external systems.
Testing in sandboxes is not recommended.

content: An admin opens MuleSoft Composer and wants to create a new flow from scratch. Which option should they select on the home page?
An analyst wants to understand how MuleSoft Composer helps **reduce integration complexity** for business users. How does the documentation describe this benefit?
options: By completely hiding integration logic from the user.
By letting users experiment and iterate on a flow, breaking logic into clear steps and adjusting as they discover new use cases.
By replacing all legacy systems with Salesforce.
By preventing flows from being modified after activation.

content: Northern Trail Outfitters must automate a set of actions in Salesforce that a single user will execute after a Case is created. The solution must organize actions into **logical steps** and allow user input during the process. Which feature is most appropriate?
options: A single autolaunched flow with Apex invocations.
A Flow Orchestration with Stages and Steps to organize automated actions and user interactions.

content: An admin wants to build a **guided** process where the user interacts with screens and makes decisions. Which flow type is most appropriate?

A company must run automation automatically whenever an Account record is created or updated, **before** it is saved to the database, in order to update a calculated field. Which Flow type should be used?
In Flow Builder, when clicking **Debug**, what does the admin get that is different from **Run**?
options: Debug runs an old version of the flow.
Debug shows details of each step, including inputs, outputs, and errors, helping identify issues.
Debug logs only to external files.

content: A Flow Orchestration must involve multiple teams in different stages of a project approval process. How do **Interactive Steps** help in this scenario?
options: They allow users to interact with the process between automated backend steps, completing work items before the orchestration moves on.
They remove the need for Salesforce licenses.
They are used only for external integrations.

content: An admin wants to reuse common logic across several flows, such as validations and field updates. Which feature is recommended?
options: Subflows called from a parent flow.

content: A company wants to automate a process where a set of records must be processed in batch every day at 11:00 PM. Which flow type is most appropriate?
An architect is reviewing automations in an org. What is Salesforce’s current recommendation for new **declarative** automation?
An admin is testing a flow that is not yet active. Which version does **Run** use in Flow Builder?
options: The most recently saved version of the flow, even if it is not active.
Only versions deployed via change sets.

content: A hyperautomation process requires that a user reviews data and makes a manual decision at a certain point, after several automated steps. Where does this human interaction best fit?
options: In an Interactive Step within a Flow Orchestration.

content: A team wants to expose an external service (provided by a REST API) as an invocable action in a Salesforce flow with a declarative setup. Which platform feature should be used to register this action?
options: External Services with imported OpenAPI specifications.

content: In a hyperautomation flow, the team needs to coordinate multiple flows and human steps in a **multi‑day process** with SLAs and dependencies. Which feature is most appropriate?
An admin is seeing frequent errors in a record‑triggered flow in production. Which practice is recommended to debug without impacting end users?
options: Clone the flow to a sandbox, reproduce the scenario using Debug, then adjust logic.
Change the flow directly in production without tests.
Delete and recreate the flow from scratch.
Ignore errors; the system will self‑adjust.

content: A developer built a complex flow mixing UI logic and external integrations. To make it more sustainable, which approach aligns with best practices?
options: Keep everything in a single flow for readability.
Separate UI logic into Screen Flows, business logic into autolaunched flows, and integrations into reusable APIs.
Convert the entire flow to Apex.
Move the logic to Process Builder.
Use RPA only to interact with Salesforce.

content: In Flow Builder, what is the main difference in purpose between **Run** and **Debug**?
options: Run always uses the active version; Debug always uses an old version.
Run executes the flow normally; Debug shows detailed information per element (inputs, outputs, errors) for troubleshooting.
Run works only in production; Debug only in sandbox.
Run ignores validations; Debug applies all validations.
Run uses sample data; Debug uses only real data.

content: AnyAirlines must automate a process that retrieves data from an old website that **does not expose APIs**. Which combination of tools best fits Salesforce’s hyperautomation strategy?
options: MuleSoft RPA to interact with the website UI and MuleSoft Composer to orchestrate with Salesforce.

content: While using **RPA Recorder** in MuleSoft RPA, an analyst records a manual activity in a legacy system. What kind of information does the Recorder automatically capture to support automation?
options: Conditional decisions made by the user.
Documentation of the UI elements used (buttons, fields, etc.).

content: A company needs to create a new API‑based integration with MuleSoft. According to API‑led best practices, what should the developer’s **first step** be?
options: Create a project directly in Anypoint Studio with no specification.
Install Mule runtime locally and start coding.
Create the API specification (RAML) in Design Center, defining resources, methods, and contracts.

content: AnyAirlines is developing a new integration and wants **automated tests** integrated into the API development lifecycle. Which Anypoint Platform component best meets this need?
A company wants to deploy Anypoint Platform on top of AWS infrastructure with horizontal/vertical scalability and zero‑downtime deployments. Which deployment strategy is most appropriate?
options: Private Cloud Edition in an on‑premises data center.
Manual runtimes on isolated VMs without orchestration.

content: An RPA process in production at AnyAirlines has started to fail. According to recommended practices, what is the best way to debug the problem?
options: Deactivate the process and manually review screenshots.
Download the analysis package from RPA Manager, revert the process to the Build phase, import the package into RPA Builder, and debug there.
Modify the process directly in production.
Delete the process and recreate it.

content: A marketing team wants to run a monthly campaign for the top 10% customers with the highest loyalty points. The scenario uses API‑led connectivity and an external HTTP loyalty system. Which approach best aligns with API‑led best practices?
options: Create a single giant API accessing all systems.
Create System APIs for Customer and Loyalty, a Process API that combines the data, and an Experience API for business consumers.

content: In a hyperautomation pipeline, the team wants to expose one API for a mobile app and another for a web portal, both consuming the same backend capabilities. Which **API‑led layer** typically serves these channels?
An architect is designing how MuleSoft RPA and APIs should work together. In which situation is RPA **most appropriate**, according to hyperautomation guidance?
options: Whenever there are well‑defined REST APIs.
When the system lacks APIs or direct data access but has a stable user interface.
When the customer prefers not to use Salesforce.
When all declarative flows should be replaced.

content: A company uses MuleSoft Composer, Flow, and RPA in its hyperautomation strategy. Who is typically the **primary target user** for MuleSoft Composer within this combination?
options: Admins and business analysts who need to build SaaS‑to‑SaaS integrations quickly with a visual interface.
End users with no edit permissions.

content: An RPA job is scheduled to start daily at 10:00 AM and runs for one hour. The schedule uses a fixed interval of 45 minutes between job starts. Assuming no failures, at what time will the process run for the **second time**?
options: 11:45 AM, because the next run can only start 45 minutes after the first job completes.

content: An integration team must monitor and manage APIs, including security policies, SLAs, and analytics. Which Anypoint Platform component is most appropriate for this function?
An organization wants to publish reusable assets—such as API specifications, templates, and connectors—for other teams to consume. Which feature should they use?
In the MuleSoft Hyperautomation Developer context, which is a typical responsibility when working on **API governance**?
options: Configuring open, unauthenticated access for ease of use.
Defining authentication standards, restricting access with policies, and monitoring usage via API Manager.
Leaving security entirely to the network team.

content: A company wants to ensure that only authorized users can trigger automations involving sensitive data in Salesforce, Composer, and RPA. Which strategy is most appropriate?
options: Give everyone the System Administrator profile.
Control access via Salesforce profiles/permission sets, limit who can configure Composer connections, and use access control in RPA Manager.

content: In a hyperautomation scenario, the team wants to reduce coupling between front ends and backend systems so internal changes do not break external channels. Which API‑led layer directly supports this objective?
An architect is deciding between MuleSoft Composer and a full Anypoint Platform‑based integration. In which scenario is Composer **most appropriate**?
options: Highly customized integrations with complex performance and governance requirements.
Simple SaaS‑to‑SaaS integrations that must be delivered quickly with minimal coding.
On‑premises implementations with no cloud access.
Scenarios requiring deep unit testing with MUnit.
Situations where a visual interface is not desired.

content: A company wants to use MuleSoft RPA and Composer in a service process involving data capture in a legacy system, Salesforce record updates, and Slack notifications. Which design best represents the hyperautomation approach?
options: RPA does everything, without Composer or Salesforce.
Composer orchestrates the process, calling an RPA bot for the legacy system, updating Salesforce, and sending Slack notifications.

content: A team is defining KPIs to measure the success of hyperautomation initiatives. Which metric set is most relevant?
options: Number of custom objects created, number of Apex triggers.
Cycle‑time reduction, decrease in manual errors, increase in process/case throughput, and user satisfaction.

content: A hyperautomation architect needs to align business and IT stakeholders on the scope of upcoming automation waves. Which practice is most appropriate?
options: Let each department create isolated automations without alignment.
Run process discovery sessions, map end‑to‑end journeys, and prioritize high‑impact use cases aligned with the API and automation roadmap.
Start with technical implementation only, without involving business.
Choose only easy processes, regardless of value.
Focus only on internal integrations, ignoring customer experience.
